name: Svg.Path

unions.name: Path
unions.comment:
Commands for writing Paths.

You might want to use `PathBuilder` to build paths.
cases: { radiusX : Basics.Float, radiusY : Basics.Float, rotation : Basics.Float, takeTheLongWay : Basics.Bool, clockwise : Basics.Bool }

aliases.name: PathBuilder
aliases.comment:
Builds Paths.

Start building paths with `startAt` and end it with `end`.

values.name: custom
values.comment:
custom path command.

You should only use this if you know what your doing.

    ```
    startAt (50,50)
    |> custom (\(x,y) -> ((x+10,y+10),"l10 10"))
    |> end
    ```
values.type: (( Basics.Float, Basics.Float ) -> ( ( Basics.Float, Basics.Float ), String.String )) -> Svg.Path.PathBuilder -> Svg.Path.PathBuilder

values.name: drawArcBy
values.comment: draw an arc to a relative point
values.type: ( Basics.Float, Basics.Float ) -> { radiusX : Basics.Float, radiusY : Basics.Float, rotation : Basics.Float, takeTheLongWay : Basics.Bool, clockwise : Basics.Bool } -> Svg.Path.PathBuilder -> Svg.Path.PathBuilder

values.name: drawArcTo
values.comment: draw an arc to a point
values.type: ( Basics.Float, Basics.Float ) -> { radiusX : Basics.Float, radiusY : Basics.Float, rotation : Basics.Float, takeTheLongWay : Basics.Bool, clockwise : Basics.Bool } -> Svg.Path.PathBuilder -> Svg.Path.PathBuilder

values.name: drawCircleArcAround
values.comment: Draw a circle arc around a center point
values.type: ( Basics.Float, Basics.Float ) -> { angle : Basics.Float, clockwise : Basics.Bool } -> Svg.Path.PathBuilder -> Svg.Path.PathBuilder

values.name: drawCircleArcAroundBy
values.comment: draw a circle arc around a relative center
values.type: ( Basics.Float, Basics.Float ) -> { angle : Basics.Float, clockwise : Basics.Bool } -> Svg.Path.PathBuilder -> Svg.Path.PathBuilder

values.name: drawCircleArcBy
values.comment: draw a circle arc to a relative point
values.type: ( Basics.Float, Basics.Float ) -> { angle : Basics.Float, takeTheLongWay : Basics.Bool, clockwise : Basics.Bool } -> Svg.Path.PathBuilder -> Svg.Path.PathBuilder

values.name: drawCircleArcTo
values.comment: draw a circle arc to a point
values.type: ( Basics.Float, Basics.Float ) -> { angle : Basics.Float, takeTheLongWay : Basics.Bool, clockwise : Basics.Bool } -> Svg.Path.PathBuilder -> Svg.Path.PathBuilder

values.name: drawLineBy
values.comment: draw a line to a relative point

values.name: drawLineTo
values.comment: draw a line to a point

values.name: end
values.comment: end the path

values.name: endClosed
values.comment: move back to the start and end the path

values.name: jumpBy
values.comment: jump by a relative amount (without drawing)

values.name: jumpTo
values.comment: jump to a point (without drawing)

values.name: startAt
values.comment: start building a path

values.name: toString
values.comment:
Convert the path to a string.

You can use it with elm/svg like this:

        Svg.path
            [ Svg.Attributes.d (toString path)]
            []

name: Svg.Writer

unions.name: SvgNode
unions.comment:
A Svg Node.

It can be turned into a string using `toString` or into html using `toHtml`.
cases: { name : String.String, attributes : List.List ( String.String, String.String ), content : List.List Svg.Writer.SvgNode }

aliases.name: Program
aliases.comment: A Program for viewing your Svg creations

values.name: circle
values.comment: define a cirlce svg node
values.type: { radius : Basics.Float, pos : ( Basics.Float, Basics.Float ) } -> Svg.Writer.SvgNode

values.name: define
values.comment:
define a template.

You can use a template with `use`.

values.name: defineMask
values.comment:
define a mask.

You can use the mask with `withMask`.

values.name: group
values.comment: group nodes to apply styling to all nodes at once.

values.name: path
values.comment: turn a path into a svg node.

values.name: rectangle
values.comment: create a rectangle svg node
values.type: { topLeft : ( Basics.Float, Basics.Float ), width : Basics.Float, height : Basics.Float } -> Svg.Writer.SvgNode

values.name: toDataURI
values.comment:
turn the list of svg nodes into a data URI.

    backgroundImage : Html.Attribute msg
    backgroundImage =
        Html.Attributes.style "background-image"
            ( "url(\\""
                ++ toDataURI { width = 100, height = 100 }
                    svgNodes
                ++ "\\")"
            )
values.type: { width : Basics.Int, height : Basics.Int } -> List.List Svg.Writer.SvgNode -> String.String

values.name: toHtml
values.comment:
convert the svg nodes into html.

Note: the svg will be displayed as the background of a div.

I tried converting it into a Svg node, but my browser didn't seem to like it.

Alternatively i looked into elm/Svg, but there i can't define custom attributes.
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { width : Basics.Int, height : Basics.Int } -> List.List Svg.Writer.SvgNode -> Html.Html msg

values.name: toProgram
values.comment:
Convert into a program for viewing your Svg creations.

You might want to use this in combination with hot-loading, while you write your svg.
values.type: { name : String.String, width : Basics.Int, height : Basics.Int } -> List.List Svg.Writer.SvgNode -> Svg.Writer.Program

values.name: toString
values.comment: Convert the list of svg nodes into a svg-string.
values.type: { width : Basics.Int, height : Basics.Int } -> List.List Svg.Writer.SvgNode -> String.String

values.name: use
values.comment: Use a defined template

values.name: withAttribute
values.comment:
@Deprecated

Use `withCustomAttribute` instead.

values.name: withCustomAttribute
values.comment: add a attribute

values.name: withFillColor
values.comment: fill the shape with a color

values.name: withMask
values.comment: Use a defined mask

values.name: withNoFillColor
values.comment: use no color for the filling

values.name: withNoStrokeColor
values.comment: don't draw the border of the shape

values.name: withStrokeColor
values.comment: use a color for the border of the shape

values.name: withStrokeWidth
values.comment: define the with of the border of the shape
